description_long: This is a small, single-story wooden cabin with a gable roof covered in brown, shingle-like tiles. The exterior walls are clad in horizontal light wood paneling with darker wood trim at the corners and around the windows and doors. The front facade features a covered porch area supported by simple posts, providing a sheltered entryway. There is a double-paned rectangular window with a white frame on the front and side, allowing natural light into the interior. The rear and one side of the cabin are solid, with no visible windows or doors, emphasizing privacy and insulation. The overall design is compact and functional, suitable for residential or recreational use in an urban or suburban setting.
description: A compact wooden cabin with a gable shingle roof, light wood siding, a covered front porch, and a double-paned window.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, main entrance, and a double-paned window with white trim.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying a solid wall with no windows or doors, and the gable roof's peak.
description_view_2: Side view with a continuous light wood wall and no openings, highlighting the cabin's length.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the double-paned window and the porch area extending from the front.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate pitch, covered in brown asphalt shingles and featuring slight overhangs on all sides
attribute_window_arrangement: One double-paned rectangular window with a white frame on the front right and one on the right side, both of medium size and symmetrically placed relative to the porch
attribute_door_placement: Single main entrance door located on the front facade, opening onto the covered porch area
